event: Jeff and Shirley discover a shared childhood: at a mall foosball table a child bully ('Big Cheddar') once thrashed a little boy ('Tinkletown') so mercilessly that he wet his pants in front of the laughing kids and quit foosball forever. Both turn out to be in the room now.
ground_truth_evidence: VERIFIED against Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foosball_and_Nocturnal_Vigilantism). "an incident from her childhood in which she (then known as 'Big Cheddar') trashed a boy (derisively called 'Tinkletown') at foosball and made him pee his pants by jabbing him with the foosball bar in the crotch." ... "Jeff reveals that he was Tinkletown."
cold_prompts: In Community's foosball episode, which member of the study group turns out to have been the childhood bully who once humiliated Jeff at foosball until he wet his pants?
There's a Community episode where a flashback reveals which study-group member was the childhood foosball bully that made young Jeff wet himself. Who was it?
correct_premise_prompt: In Community, why did Shirley bully young Jeff so badly at foosball that he peed his pants?
lure_premise_prompt: In Community, why did Pierce turn out to be the kid who bullied young Jeff at foosball until he wet himself?
foil_premise_prompt: In Community, how did Abed end up being the childhood bully who made young Jeff wet his pants at foosball?
notes: S3 character-backstory beat, not a marketed twist. WHO was the bully is genuinely mis-schematized: viewers default to the group's established mean character (Pierce, the casually bigoted bullying old man; Chang is a secondary fit), not the sweet, sing-song church-mom Shirley, whose buried ruthless/competitive streak is the joke. F5 clean (no 'well actually' correction genre). Mild F1 risk: Community is cult-popular but less mass-market than The Simpsons/The Office, so if the model doesn't know the episode it should hedge rather than confabulate; dossier flags for the obscurity screen. Foil Abed (detached pop-culture savant) is wildly off-model as a crotch-jabbing foosball bully.
build_notes: cold_prompts[1] drafted by me in the SEIN-001 second-cold-prompt style; dossier supplied only one cold_prompt. target_entity/lure_entity given full surnames (Shirley Bennett, Pierce Hawthorne) for consistency with SEIN-001; dossier used first names in grading keywords, preserved verbatim. Air year omitted (dossier gives only S3E9); not invented.
